questions.text: Choose the most appropriate title for the passage.
questions.context: People often assume that accurate predictions depend mainly on experience. However, studies of forecasting show that experts sometimes become overly committed to their original expectations. When new evidence appears, they may unconsciously interpret it in ways that support their previous beliefs. Individuals who regularly reconsider their conclusions, by contrast, are often more successful because they adjust their judgments whenever reliable evidence changes.

options.text: Why Changing Your Mind Can Improve Judgement
The Importance of Trusting First Impressions
Why Experts Never Make Prediction Errors

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate topic of the passage.
questions.context: Throughout history, many discoveries have resulted from unexpected observations rather than careful planning alone. Scientists occasionally notice results that differ from their predictions and choose to investigate them instead of dismissing them as mistakes. Although chance may provide the initial observation, important discoveries usually occur because researchers recognise the significance of unusual evidence and examine it systematically.

options.text: how unexpected observations contribute to scientific discovery
why experiments should always follow predictions exactly

questions.text: Which statement is supported by the passage?

options.text: Scientific discoveries occur only by accident.
Unusual results may lead to important discoveries if they are investigated carefully.
Scientists rarely change their original hypotheses.

questions.text: Choose the main idea of the passage.
questions.context: Many people believe that successful communication depends primarily on speaking clearly. Communication researchers, however, argue that effective listening is equally important. Individuals who ask questions, confirm understanding, and consider another person's perspective are less likely to misunderstand complex information. As a result, successful communication requires active participation from both speakers and listeners.

options.text: Speaking is more important than listening.
Effective communication depends on active listening as well as clear speaking.
People should avoid discussing complicated topics.

questions.text: How many of the following statements are NOT consistent with the passage?
questions.context:
A university student wanted to improve her writing skills. Instead of submitting only final drafts, she began asking classmates for feedback before completing each essay. Although receiving criticism was sometimes uncomfortable, she gradually recognised recurring weaknesses and learned how to correct them. Over time, the quality of her writing improved noticeably.

Statements

(a) She asked for suggestions before finishing her essays.
(b) Feedback was always enjoyable for her.
(c) She discovered repeated weaknesses in her writing.
(d) Her writing improved after using feedback.
(e) She refused to change her writing.
(f) Constructive criticism can contribute to improvement.

questions.text: How many of the following vocabulary pairs are correctly matched?
questions.context:
(a) consistent — remaining the same
(b) scarce — abundant
(c) interpret — explain the meaning of
(d) efficient — producing good results with little waste
(e) rigid — flexible
(f) justify — provide a good reason for
(g) diminish — become smaller
(h) temporary — permanent
(i) verify — confirm the truth of
(j) infer — reach a conclusion from evidence

questions.text: Which statement does NOT agree with the two explanations given in the passage?
questions.context: Some researchers argue that cities become centres of innovation because they bring together large numbers of talented individuals who exchange ideas easily. Others believe that innovation depends more on access to financial resources, educational institutions, and supportive government policies. Although these explanations focus on different factors, both attempt to explain why certain cities produce more inventions than others.

options.text: Large populations may encourage the exchange of ideas.
Educational institutions may contribute to technological development.
Only population size determines whether innovation occurs.
The two explanations propose different causes for the same pattern.

questions.text: Which underlined word is used incorrectly?

options.text: The committee reached a _compromise_ after lengthy negotiations.
The company decided to _expand_ its international operations.
The engineer designed the building to remain _unstable_ during earthquakes.

questions.text: Which meaning best matches the underlined expression?
questions.context: People who demonstrate _critical thinking_ evaluate evidence carefully before reaching conclusions. They compare different explanations, recognise weak arguments, and remain willing to change their opinions if stronger evidence becomes available.

options.text: evaluating information carefully before making judgments

questions.text: Which underlined word is NOT appropriate in context?
questions.context: Successful investigations require researchers to collect accurate data, examine alternative explanations, and report their findings honestly. Scientists must remain ① _objective_ throughout the research process and avoid allowing personal opinions to influence their conclusions. Publishing misleading information would therefore be ② _ethical_ rather than scientific. Reliable research depends upon ③ _careful_ observation, ④ _systematic_ analysis, and ⑤ _independent_ verification.

questions.text: Choose the word that best completes the blank.
questions.context: Students often believe that making mistakes indicates failure. Educational psychologists, however, argue that errors become valuable learning opportunities when students examine them carefully and modify their study methods. Reflection is therefore an ________ part of effective learning.

questions.text: Which sentence best fits the blank?
questions.context:
Experienced musicians rarely become satisfied with simply repeating pieces they already perform well. Instead, they identify passages that remain difficult and practise those sections repeatedly until they improve. ______

Consequently, their performance continues to develop over many years.

options.text: Deliberately practising weaknesses is more effective than repeating strengths.
Practice becomes unnecessary after professional success.
Difficult practice usually reduces confidence without improving performance.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate order of the following sentences.
questions.context:
(A) After comparing several explanations, they identified the factor responsible for the unexpected result.

(B) Researchers noticed that one experiment produced results that differed from previous studies.

(C) Rather than ignoring the difference, they repeated the experiment using additional controls.

questions.text: Choose the sentence that does NOT belong.
questions.context: Scientific research depends on careful observation and honest reporting.

options.text: Careful experiments reduce the risk of error.
Ancient castles were often surrounded by defensive walls.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate word for the blank.
questions.context: Effective teams recognise that disagreement can improve decision-making when it is expressed respectfully. Members who question assumptions often identify problems before they become serious. Constructive discussion therefore promotes ________ rather than conflict.

questions.text: Which statement is supported by the passage?
questions.context: Researchers studying sleep have found that memory is strengthened not only while people are awake but also during sleep. During certain stages of sleep, the brain reorganises recently learned information, making it easier to remember later. Although sleeping cannot replace studying, adequate rest significantly improves learning efficiency.

options.text: Memory can improve while people are asleep.
Only long hours of studying improve memory.
Sleep has little effect on learning.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate title for the passage.
questions.context: People often believe that successful habits depend mainly on self-discipline. Behavioural scientists, however, have shown that small environmental changes can make desirable behaviours easier to perform. Placing healthy food where it is visible, preparing exercise clothes in advance, or reducing distractions while studying all increase the likelihood that positive habits will continue.

options.text: How Small Environmental Changes Encourage Good Habits
Why Successful People Never Face Temptation
The Relationship Between Intelligence and Habit Formation

questions.text: Choose the word that best completes the blank.
questions.context: The museum decided to restore the damaged painting immediately because exposure to moisture would have caused the damage to become even more ________.

questions.text: Which sentence best summarises the passage?
questions.context: Economic systems consist of many interconnected relationships among consumers, businesses, governments, and financial institutions. Changes affecting one group frequently influence others in unexpected ways. As a result, economists often analyse the entire system instead of focusing on individual factors alone.

options.text: Modern economies are interconnected, so individual changes may affect many different groups.
Businesses always benefit from economic change.
Consumers have little influence on economic activity.

questions.text: Choose the word that best completes the passage.
questions.context: Researchers frequently revise their conclusions after collecting additional information. Rather than viewing revision as failure, scientists consider it evidence that the research process is working effectively. Scientific knowledge therefore continues to ________ as new evidence becomes available.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate sentence to fill the blank.
questions.context: People often believe that confidence automatically produces success. However, psychologists have found that confidence alone is rarely enough. Successful individuals regularly evaluate their performance, identify weaknesses, and adjust their strategies accordingly. ______

options.text: Continuous improvement depends on honest self-evaluation.
Mistakes should be ignored whenever possible.
Successful people avoid changing their methods.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate order of the following paragraphs.
questions.context:
(A) They then tested a new explanation using additional experiments.

(B) Scientists noticed that the existing theory could not explain recent observations.

(C) The revised theory successfully accounted for both the original and the new evidence.

questions.text: Which statement is NOT supported by the passage?
questions.context: Studies suggest that neighbourhoods with strong social connections are often safer because residents communicate regularly and are more willing to report suspicious activity or assist one another. Researchers argue that cooperation strengthens communities even before problems arise.

options.text: Social cooperation may improve neighbourhood safety.
Strong communities often help prevent problems.
Community cooperation becomes valuable only after crime occurs.
Residents who know one another are often more willing to help.

questions.text: Choose the word that best completes the passage.
questions.context: Because technology changes rapidly, employees are expected to update their knowledge throughout their careers. Employers therefore increasingly value people who demonstrate curiosity, flexibility, and a willingness to continue ________.

questions.text: Choose the most appropriate title for the passage.
questions.context: Many organisations encourage employees to discuss unsuccessful projects openly. Rather than assigning blame, managers analyse what happened and identify ways to improve future planning. This approach helps organisations avoid repeating mistakes while encouraging innovation and continuous improvement.

options.text: Why Successful Companies Never Experience Failure
Competition Is the Only Key to Success

questions.text: Complete the summary using the words from the box.
questions.context:
**Word Box: feedback · evidence · confidence · revise · assumptions**

People sometimes become overly (A) __________ in their own opinions. As a result, they may ignore new (B) __________ that contradicts their previous (C) __________. Successful thinkers instead seek (D) __________ from others and remain willing to (E) __________ their conclusions when necessary.

questions.text: Choose the word that is closest in meaning to the underlined word.
questions.context: The company decided to _modify_ its marketing strategy after reviewing customer feedback.

questions.text: Arrange the following words and phrases into one grammatically correct sentence.
questions.context:
Arrange all of the following words and phrases into one complete sentence. You may not change the form of any word.

(the more / students / reflect on / their mistakes / the more / they / improve / their understanding)

questions.text: Choose the grammatically correct sentence.

options.text: Not until the report was published did the public understand the problem.
Not until the report was published the public did understand the problem.
Not until did the report was published the public understand the problem.
Not until the report published did the public understood the problem.
Not until the report was published the public understood the problem.

questions.text: Complete both tasks below.
questions.context:
Task 4.1 — Rewrite the sentence using the word given.

Word: despite

Although the engineer encountered several unexpected problems, she completed the project successfully.

Task 4.2 — Arrange all of the following words and phrases into one complete sentence. You may not change the form of any word.

(only after / had / carefully / the researchers / analysed / the data / did / they / reach / their conclusion)

questions.text:
Write 2–3 English sentences answering the following question.

Several passages in this exam suggest that people improve by examining mistakes, accepting feedback, and changing their ideas.

Do you agree?

Support your answer using one idea from the passages and one example from your own experience.
